h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep trying to make that 4 count beats too
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue attempting to place four tiles in the bar to match the bottom number.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: writes 3/8 below the bar, then puts the quarter-note card back over the lower box... Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the card swap and correctly deduces the changing time signature without adding extra tiles.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to isolate the variables and prevent the learner from conflating the beat count with the beat unit.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The worksheet now shows the separation: top number, one bar; bottom number, one beat unit. Mark the next bar with tiles first. Then change only the beat-unit card and say which number can change.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's successful separation of concepts and advances the task to testing variable changes.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's successful separation of concepts and advances the task to testing variable changes.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to isolate the variables and prevent the learner from conflating the beat count with the beat unit.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical covering mechanism to isolate the variables and prevent the learner from conflating the beat count with the beat unit.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to successfully apply the object-first rule to each subsequent arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently uses the established criterion to evaluate conditional physical possibilities for an ambiguous arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a strategic reversal by the tutor.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Object first: track on cart, if this is the upward push. Then the arrow direction is up. If this slot is the down arrow, it is Earth on cart instead.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently uses the established criterion to evaluate conditional physical possibilities for an ambiguous arrow.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a strategic reversal by the tutor.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a strategic reversal by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue trying to refine the wording of the metadata note to perfectly capture the column's meaning.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new sentence-check device and uses it to explicitly rule out the false claims they were previously making.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The header names the field; the row supplies one value. It does not summarize the column. 09:14 check: for `city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May`, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor recognizes that defining the header is insufficient and introduces a new public mechanism (reading the row as a sentence) to shift the learner's cognitive focus.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new sentence-check device and uses it to explicitly rule out the false claims they were previously making.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor recognizes that defining the header is insufficient and introduces a new public mechanism (reading the row as a sentence) to shift the learner's cognitive focus.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor recognizes that defining the header is insufficient and introduces a new public mechanism (reading the row as a sentence) to shift the learner's cognitive focus.
